own_review.issue: gluonhq/setup-graalvm GUI step hardcodes arch:'amd64'. Docker executables are released only for amd64, so this is unnecessary now (human's point); also inconsistent with build.yml which passes x86_64/aarch64.
gluonhq/setup-graalvm@master is an unpinned mutable branch ref (vs graalvm/setup-graalvm@v1 used for CLI). Non-reproducible builds / supply-chain risk; pin to a tag or SHA.

New reachability-metadata.json added but legacy reflect-config.json (14406B) and jni-config.json (6031B) NOT removed (verified present at BASE). GraalVM auto-discovers and merges all META-INF/native-image configs, so reflection/JNI metadata now exists in two formats and the legacy copies will drift stale.

Root locale "" dropped from bundles for QuantumMessagesBundle, XMLEntities and controls (was ["","en-US","und"], now ["en-US","und"]). Verified by diffing base vs new. Low real impact but a genuine change to default-locale fallback.

The new globs section is an INCOMPLETE mirror of resources.includes: DriftyMainLight.png, SplashLight.png and the frags DrawRoundRect_Color/Blend_SRC_IN/LinearConvolveShadow_20/LinearConvolveShadow_4 appear in includes but not in globs. Verified by parsing the new JSON: HARMLESS at runtime because the includes section retains every one of these entries and GraalVM merges includes+globs. It is only an internal inconsistency, not a dropped resource. FillRoundRect_Color.frag is present (in both sections).
Unchanged by PR but still references icudt72b only; the GUI config added icudt74b for JDK23. CLI native config not updated to the JDK23 ICU data version.
Parent pom defines both maven.compiler.source/target properties AND a maven-compiler-plugin with explicit <source>/<target>; each module repeats the properties. Redundant duplication.
JavaFX left on an EA build while moving to stable Java 23; project docs still reference Java 21 / GraalVM 21 (PR checklist leaves docs box unchecked).

The headline 'shader files dropped during reformat' alarm is FALSE. All shaders (DrawRoundRect_Color, Blend_SRC_IN, LinearConvolveShadow_20, LinearConvolveShadow_4, FillRoundRect_Color) are present in resource-config.json's includes section in the NEW file. Any claim that they are absent/will fail at runtime is a fabrication driven by reading only the globs section.

points.claim: 4 shader frags dropped during reformat, absent from BOTH includes and globs -> rendering crashes
points.reason: FABRICATION. Verified: all 4 frags are present in the new file's resources.includes section; only globs omits some of them. 'absent from both sections' is verifiably false and is framed as a critical runtime risk.
Verified true by diffing base vs new bundles.

points.claim: SplashLight.png in includes but missing from globs
points.reason: Factually correct about the globs omission (most accurate of the resource-config observations). 'won't be bundled' conclusion is overstated but the observation is real.

points.claim: Windows profile overrides gluonfx to 1.0.23, so Windows GUI not on upgraded 1.0.25 despite PR claim of fixing all 3 OS
points.reason: Verified in GUI/pom.xml profile; valid PR-claim-vs-reality point.

points.claim: Legacy reflect-config.json + jni-config.json not cleaned up; GraalVM merges both
points.reason: Verified both exist at base; valid duplication concern.

points.claim: CodeQL/Formatter use Oracle JDK 23; verify OpenRewrite (mvn rewrite:run) compat
points.reason: Verified workflows use distribution oracle + rewrite:run; mild but valid.

points.claim: Redundant per-module maven.compiler.source/target vs parent compiler plugin
points.reason: Verified redundancy; mechanism description slightly imprecise but observation valid.

exceeds: Identified the bundles root-locale drop and the Windows 1.0.23 override, both genuine and verified

points.claim: DriftyMainLight.png + SplashLight.png missing from globs -> light theme/splash will fail to load at runtime
points.reason: Globs omission is real, but the asserted runtime failure is FALSE: both are present in resources.includes and GraalVM merges includes+globs. Framed as a regression that does not exist.

points.claim: 4 shader frags removed from globs; loader classes retained but resources absent -> NPE/MissingResourceException
points.reason: Loader classes (DrawRoundRect_Color_Loader, PPSBlend_SRC_INPeer) do exist in reachability-metadata, but the frags are present in resources.includes, so 'resources absent -> NPE at runtime' is a fabrication. The strongest-stated point is wrong.

points.claim: CLI resource-config.json still on icudt72b, not updated to icudt74b for JDK23
points.reason: Verified: CLI config (unchanged) references icudt72b; real JDK23 ICU inconsistency on a relevant file. Failure-mechanism wording is muddled but the finding is legitimate and unique.

points.claim: CLI native-maven-plugin stuck at 0.10.4 across all 4 profiles, predates Java 23
points.reason: Verified version in CLI/pom.xml (4 occurrences of 0.10.4); valid compatibility concern.

points.claim: Windows profile hardcodes 1.0.23 as property value vs base property; suggest separate windows property
macos-13/macos-14 runners + arch selection correct, no issues observed
points.reason: Non-finding (trivial); macos-13 does not even appear in the diff.

points.claim: Docs still list Java 21 / GraalVM 21; PR docs checklist box should be checked
points.reason: Matches own review docs-stale point; valid.

missed: Legacy reflect-config.json + jni-config.json not cleaned up (own review)
exceeds: Unique catches the others missed: CLI icudt72b not bumped to icudt74b, and CLI native-maven-plugin 0.10.4 not assessed for Java 23

qualitative: v8 (docker rebuild of the v7 harness) regressed in quality on PR #801. v7's headline 'shader dropped' point is a single fabrication (the frags are present in resources.includes; only globs omits them), but its other seven points are all verified-good, including the bundles root-locale drop, the @master pin, the legacy reflect/jni duplication, and the Windows 1.0.23 override. v8 trades those solid CI/config catches for two confidently-wrong resource-config 'will fail at runtime' claims that ignore the includes section, doubling the absence fabrications to two. v8's redeeming, genuinely-unique finds are the unchanged CLI config still on icudt72b and the CLI native-maven-plugin frozen at 0.10.4, which v7 missed entirely. Net: v7 stays close to the ground-truth review while v8 drifts into hallucinated runtime regressions, so v8 is the weaker run despite a couple of original observations.
